forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+color: e36209
+description: 'Fix KV-cache prefix reuse for hybrid/recurrent models (Qwen3.6-35B-A3B MoE, review.model: qwen). Root cause: find_best_prefix_match (crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:224) ranks donors by LCP alone, so a sibling fork with a marginally longer LCP is preferred over the pinned zero-rollback prime donor. On recurrent models the sibling requires a ~670-token recurrent-state rollback that exceeds n_rs_seq=64 (model.rs:436), so seq_rm returns Ok(false) and the cache is discarded → full cold prefill on every review call. The prime turn already snapshots exactly the shared prefix (fleet.rs prime + pin_on_save), so the fix is donor SELECTION, not snapshot timing. Make selection rollback-aware: a donor''s value is the tokens that actually survive the trim (lcp iff donor_len-lcp <= recurrent_window, else unusable). Entirely below ACP — no protocol change; pin_on_save/_meta and session/fork already exist. Known-issue context: llama.cpp ggml-org issues #18497, #19794 (closed not-planned), #22450 (our exact model, can hang), #20225, #22746 — partial recurrent rollback is treated as a structural limitation upstream; vLLM/SGLang solve it with purpose-built hybrid KV managers. We avoid the rollback instead.'
+name: Hybrid KV Prefix Reuse
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,3 +0,0 @@
-color: e36209
-description: 'Fix KV-cache prefix reuse for hybrid/recurrent models (Qwen3.6-35B-A3B MoE, review.model: qwen). Root cause: find_best_prefix_match (crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:224) ranks donors by LCP alone, so a sibling fork with a marginally longer LCP is preferred over the pinned zero-rollback prime donor. On recurrent models the sibling requires a ~670-token recurrent-state rollback that exceeds n_rs_seq=64 (model.rs:436), so seq_rm returns Ok(false) and the cache is discarded → full cold prefill on every review call. The prime turn already snapshots exactly the shared prefix (fleet.rs prime + pin_on_save), so the fix is donor SELECTION, not snapshot timing. Make selection rollback-aware: a donor''s value is the tokens that actually survive the trim (lcp iff donor_len-lcp <= recurrent_window, else unusable). Entirely below ACP — no protocol change; pin_on_save/_meta and session/fork already exist. Known-issue context: llama.cpp ggml-org issues #18497, #19794 (closed not-planned), #22450 (our exact model, can hang), #20225, #22746 — partial recurrent rollback is treated as a structural limitation upstream; vLLM/SGLang solve it with purpose-built hybrid KV managers. We avoid the rollback instead.'
-name: Hybrid KV Prefix Reuse
